content:
- Verified post-switch Ralph state: .ralph/current_task.txt is intentionally absent after .ralph/task_switch.sh, and .ralph/current_tasks.md now lists the new schema-contract bug first.
- Cleaned up the Compose stack with docker compose down -v after reproducing the bug.
- Next context should refresh Ralph summaries, select the new bug task into .ralph/current_task.txt, and fix the schema-contract drift before returning to story-99 manual verification.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read choose-task rules plus the done-task summary.
- Confirmed the active backlog contains one unblocked failing high-priority bug (`.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-benchmark-schema-contract-drifted-to-bench.md`) and one unblocked failing non-bug manual verification task; bug-first rule makes the schema-contract bug the required next pick.
- Sanity-checked the actual repo identity from codebase files: Go module `pg_gobench` with `go test`, `go vet`, `gofmt` linting, plus Docker Compose and Kubernetes example manifests. Next context should work the selected bug task, then resume story-99 manual verification only after the bug passes.

- Read previous Ralph progress, active task pointer, and the `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active work item is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-benchmark-schema-contract-drifted-to-bench.md`; next step is to inspect that task and decide whether this is a planning-only pass or an execution pass.
- Need to read the bug task contents, then inspect the relevant code and tests before making any edits.

- Determined this turn must be planning-only because the active bug task has no `NOW EXECUTE` marker.
- Confirmed the core bug shape: `internal/benchrunner` already uses schema `bench` to avoid PostgreSQL's reserved `pg_` prefix, while story/manual-verification artifacts still require `pg_gobench`.
- Next step is to write a dedicated bug plan plus update the bug task with a `<plan>` pointer, then stop immediately as instructed.

- Created the planning artifact for `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-benchmark-schema-contract-drifted-to-bench.md` at `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-benchmark-schema-contract-drifted-to-bench_plans/2026-04-30-benchmark-schema-contract-plan.md`.
- Added the `<plan>` pointer to the bug task and promoted the new plan from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Key design choice for the next context: keep `bench` as the only benchmark schema contract, keep `pg_gobench` for app identity only, start execution with a failing contract-artifact Go test, then update stale task/plan artifacts and manually verify the Compose stack.

- Read the active task pointer and confirmed the current work item is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-benchmark-schema-contract-drifted-to-bench.md`.
- Re-read the `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill files from disk to follow the required workflow for this turn.
- Next I am reading the bug task plus its linked plan to execute the red-green steps if the plan still ends in `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Read the active bug task and its linked plan, which ends in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn is an execution pass.
- The first execution step is a narrow RED contract test over the live schema-spec artifacts, not a repo-wide string rename.
- I am now scanning the relevant files for stale `pg_gobench` schema expectations and the existing runtime `bench` contract so the first test targets only the live boundary.

- Added one narrow RED test at `internal/benchrunner/contract_artifacts_test.go` that reads only the four live schema-contract artifact files.
- The test requires those files to describe the benchmark schema as `bench` and fails on stale `pg_gobench` schema phrases or `pg_gobench.<table>` qualifiers.
- Next I am running that test alone to lock in the red state before updating any artifacts.

- Updated the four live benchmark-schema contract artifacts to use `bench` and cleaned the stale non-historical setup error literal in `internal/benchmarkrun/coordinator_test.go`.
- This keeps `internal/benchrunner` as the only code owner of the schema while removing the second stale contract owner in task artifacts.
- Next I am rerunning the narrow contract test before moving to real Compose verification.

- The narrow red-green slice is green.
- I am now rerunning the real Docker Compose reproduction from the bug task to verify the live runtime still creates only `bench.*` tables.
- If the live stack exposes a remaining mismatch, I will add one new failing test before any further code changes; otherwise I will proceed to the required make gates.

- Manual verification against the real Docker Compose stack passed: benchmark start succeeded and PostgreSQL exposed benchmark-owned tables only under `bench.*`.
- Required short gates started after verification: `make check` passed and `make lint` passed.
- Next I am running `make test`; if it passes I will update the bug task artifacts, switch tasks, commit all changes, push, and stop.

- Completed the schema-contract bug fix: added a narrow contract-artifact test, updated the live schema-spec artifacts to `bench`, and cleaned the stale non-historical coordinator error literal.
- Manual Compose verification passed and the required repo gates passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.
- Next I am running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then I will stage all files, commit with task-finished evidence, push, and stop.
